title: 有無人同我一樣入左U之後政治睇法經歷左好大變化....變得愈來愈藍...

replies.msg:
我想好多人會負評，但我想誠實講自己想法，睇下有無人同我一樣。

入U前：英美 = democracy beacon，中共好差，dictatorship一定係差，香港咁多問題歸根究底無民主，有左民主香港會好宜居。
入U後：香港除左無民主仲有一大堆問題，英美虛偽到令人作嘔，美國周圍侵略人好多唔見得光既野信唔過，中共仲係好衰但衰得來又覺得佢真小人對佢無咁反感。好confused 自己應該信邊面，兩面都係propaganda睇唔到曙光。

我自己都唔知點解有呢個改變....
唯一無變既就係同情因為反送中要坐監既學生同同年人。

quote.msg:
then your U life is a piece of shit
US and UK may not be that great as you initially "imagine ", or even far worse than what you expect.
This should not make you think you need to be a bad ass

After getting into Uni, you still have such a binary thinking.
Your U life should be only about getting laid with hall chick, or shitting around

replies.msg: If you would scrutinise my post word by word, you should be able to observe at no point I am promoting/indulging in a dichotomous thinking, and I never want to sympathise with the CCP (although I did point out its "viciousness" pales in its affectiveness when I realise the US also committed numerous hegemonic acts). The whole post is only meant to articulate my confusion as to finding the truth in conflicting narratives (which often are accompanied by ostensibly sound evidence), and that without real power and knowledge one seems to be reduced to automaton fed with propaganda. It's never meant to say the US is bad and therefore the CCP is a lesser evil and should be embraced, OK?

replies.msg: 拉撚完未？想睇下comment都要比你呢D pk煩住晒

quote.msg: 你都好作嘔, 成家下世做中國人啦屌你老母閪

replies.msg: 應該等佢做新疆人感受吓

我以前都有過類似想法，覺得做中國人好光榮，要講普通話，國家變強我們明天會更好等等
後尾同你相反，入咗大學先諗得通

首先樓主要知道你係一個香港人
唔理你覺得自己係咪香港人，中國人都會當你係香港人
咁任何一個有腦嘅香港人都要明白，中國越好，香港就越差越冇運行，vice versa
所以任何一個香港人都唔應該希望中國發展，最好中國永遠落後
正苦講咩國家發展帶動香港係昆你架，發展完仲邊洗你香港？過河抽板都得，有著數都益自己友深圳先啦，點解要分比你香港人？

如果你身份認同係香港人，以後會留係香港，根據以上理論，你支持中國只會令香港更差，你點解要支持中國？
就算你身份認同係中國人，支那人都會當你係香港人，你返到支那都會比人歧視，你點解要支持中國？

quote.msg: 唔使試水溫啦，行埋一邊食屎啦
我真係唔係打手，但真心想問無其他人覺得除左中共，英美西方都好假？

replies.msg:

pinned.msg: If you would scrutinise my post word by word, you should be able to observe at no point I am promoting/indulging in a dichotomous thinking, and I never want to sympathise with the CCP (although I did point out its "viciousness" pales in its affectiveness when I realise the US also committed numerous hegemonic acts). The whole post is only meant to articulate my confusion as to finding the truth in conflicting narratives (which often are accompanied by ostensibly sound evidence), and that without real power and knowledge one seems to be reduced to automaton fed with propaganda. It's never meant to say the US is bad and therefore the CCP is a lesser evil and should be embraced, OK?
